text:
Market today: Geopolitical jitters from Iran initially spooked bond markets, pushing rates up. However, oil prices falling suggest de-escalation. Meanwhile, chip stocks got a boost from Micron news and AI data center prospects. This creates a push-pull for your tech-heavy book.

Your holdings: SOXL (-21.59%) and SPAL (-22.06%) are leveraged and below their stops, needing immediate attention. PTNM (-15.25%) is also below its stop. Margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep an eye on bond yields as Iran tensions fluctuate; higher rates hurt growth. Also, watch for follow-through on chip sector strength.

Bottom line: Protect your capital on leveraged positions while staying alert for chip sector opportunities.
plays: SOXL: Respect the 200 stop. Given its leveraged nature and your margin, consider trimming if it breaks to protect capital.
SPAL: This leveraged ETF is already below its 21.38 stop. Consider exiting to limit further decay and margin exposure.
AMAT: The chip sector has tailwinds. If you're looking to add, consider AMAT near its scanner entry of 606.53-605.92, with a stop at 602.66.
